---
title: "Awesome *De Novo* Peptide Sequencing"
subtitle: "An interactive, data-driven map of deep-learning approaches — not a historical survey of the field."
execute:
  echo: false
  freeze: false
---

```{python}
#| label: load-data
#| echo: false
#| output: false
import sqlite3
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

DB = Path(__file__).parent / "denovo.db" if "__file__" in dir() else Path("denovo.db")
conn = sqlite3.connect(DB)

pubs = pd.read_sql(
    """
    SELECT p.id, p.title, p.publication_date AS date, p.publication_type AS type,
           p.publisher, p.journal, p.doi, p.url,
           (SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(a.name, ', ')
              FROM publication_author pa JOIN author a ON a.id = pa.author_id
              WHERE pa.publication_id = p.id
              ORDER BY pa.author_order) AS authors,
           (SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(alg.name, ', ')
              FROM publication_algorithm pal JOIN algorithm alg ON alg.id = pal.algorithm_id
              WHERE pal.publication_id = p.id) AS models,
           (SELECT alg.repository
              FROM publication_algorithm pal JOIN algorithm alg ON alg.id = pal.algorithm_id
              WHERE pal.publication_id = p.id LIMIT 1) AS repo
    FROM publication p
    ORDER BY p.publication_date
    """,
    conn,
)
pubs["year"] = pd.to_datetime(pubs["date"], errors="coerce").dt.year

top_authors = pd.read_sql(
    """
    SELECT a.name, COUNT(pa.publication_id) AS publications
    FROM author a JOIN publication_author pa ON a.id = pa.author_id
    GROUP BY a.id
    ORDER BY publications DESC
    LIMIT 20
    """,
    conn,
)

geo = pd.read_sql(
    """
    SELECT c.name AS country,
           COUNT(DISTINCT af.id) AS affiliations,
           COUNT(DISTINCT aa.author_id) AS authors
    FROM country c
    JOIN affiliation af ON af.country_id = c.id
    LEFT JOIN author_affiliation aa ON aa.affiliation_id = af.id
    GROUP BY c.id
    ORDER BY authors DESC
    """,
    conn,
)

institutions = pd.read_sql(
    """
    SELECT af.name AS institution, c.name AS country,
           COUNT(DISTINCT aa.author_id) AS authors
    FROM affiliation af
    JOIN country c ON af.country_id = c.id
    JOIN author_affiliation aa ON aa.affiliation_id = af.id
    GROUP BY af.name
    ORDER BY authors DESC
    LIMIT 15
    """,
    conn,
)

coauth_edges = pd.read_sql(
    """
    SELECT a1.name AS source, a2.name AS target, COUNT(*) AS weight
    FROM publication_author pa1
    JOIN publication_author pa2 ON pa1.publication_id = pa2.publication_id
    JOIN author a1 ON a1.id = pa1.author_id
    JOIN author a2 ON a2.id = pa2.author_id
    WHERE pa1.author_id < pa2.author_id
      AND pa1.author_id IN (
          SELECT author_id FROM publication_author
          GROUP BY author_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
      )
      AND pa2.author_id IN (
          SELECT author_id FROM publication_author
          GROUP BY author_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
      )
    GROUP BY a1.name, a2.name
    """,
    conn,
)

author_affs = pd.read_sql(
    """
    SELECT a.name AS author, aff.name AS affiliation
    FROM author a
    JOIN author_affiliation aa ON a.id = aa.author_id
    JOIN affiliation aff ON aa.affiliation_id = aff.id
    WHERE a.id IN (
        SELECT author_id FROM publication_author
        GROUP BY author_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
    )
    """,
    conn,
)

algorithms = pd.read_sql(
    """
    SELECT alg.name AS model,
           alg.algorithm_family AS family,
           alg.short_description AS description,
           alg.repository AS repo,
           MIN(p.publication_date) AS first_pub
    FROM algorithm alg
    LEFT JOIN publication_algorithm pal ON alg.id = pal.algorithm_id
    LEFT JOIN publication p ON pal.publication_id = p.id
    GROUP BY alg.id
    ORDER BY first_pub
    """,
    conn,
)

venues = pd.read_sql(
    """
    SELECT journal AS venue, publication_type AS type, COUNT(*) AS papers
    FROM publication
    WHERE journal IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY journal, publication_type
    ORDER BY papers DESC
    """,
    conn,
)

conn.close()

ojs_define(
    pubs=pubs,
    top_authors=top_authors,
    geo=geo,
    institutions=institutions,
    coauth_edges=coauth_edges,
    author_affs=author_affs,
    algorithms=algorithms,
    venues=venues,
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
import { aq, op } from "@uwdata/arquero"

pubs_t = transpose(pubs).map(r => ({ ...r, year: +r.year, date: r.date ? new Date(r.date) : null }))
top_authors_t = transpose(top_authors)
geo_t = transpose(geo)
institutions_t = transpose(institutions)
coauth_edges_t = transpose(coauth_edges)
author_affs_t = transpose(author_affs)
algorithms_t = transpose(algorithms).map(r => ({ ...r, first_pub: r.first_pub ? new Date(r.first_pub) : null }))
venues_t = transpose(venues)

// Derived counters — everything flows from the data, no hardcoded numbers.
n_papers = pubs_t.length
n_models = algorithms_t.length
n_authors = new Set(pubs_t.flatMap(p => (p.authors ?? "").split(", ").filter(Boolean))).size
n_countries = geo_t.length
years_with_pubs = pubs_t.map(p => p.year).filter(y => Number.isFinite(y))
first_year = Math.min(...years_with_pubs)
last_year  = Math.max(...years_with_pubs)
n_preprints = pubs_t.filter(p => p.type === "preprint").length
n_peer_reviewed = pubs_t.filter(p => p.type === "peer-reviewed").length
families = Array.from(new Set(algorithms_t.map(a => a.family).filter(Boolean)))
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="hero-grid">
  <div class="hero-stat"><div class="hero-num">${n_papers}</div><div class="hero-lbl">papers</div></div>
  <div class="hero-stat"><div class="hero-num">${n_models}</div><div class="hero-lbl">models</div></div>
  <div class="hero-stat"><div class="hero-num">${n_authors}</div><div class="hero-lbl">authors</div></div>
  <div class="hero-stat"><div class="hero-num">${n_countries}</div><div class="hero-lbl">countries</div></div>
</div>`
```

```{=html}
<style>
.hero-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 1rem; margin: 1.5rem 0; }
.hero-stat { text-align: center; padding: 1rem; background: #f6f8fa; border-radius: 0.5rem; }
.hero-num { font-size: 2.5rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1f6feb; line-height: 1; }
.hero-lbl { font-size: 0.9rem; color: #57606a; margin-top: 0.25rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; }
.scope-banner { padding: 0.75rem 1rem; background: #fff8c5; border-left: 4px solid #d4a72c; border-radius: 0 0.25rem 0.25rem 0; margin: 1.5rem 0; }
</style>
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`Since **${first_year}**, **${n_papers}** papers have introduced **${n_models}** deep-learning models for *de novo* peptide sequencing, written by **${n_authors}** authors across **${n_countries}** countries. Of those papers, **${n_preprints}** are preprints and **${n_peer_reviewed}** are peer-reviewed — a snapshot of a field where the conversation moves faster than the journals.`
```

::: {.scope-banner}
**Scope.** This site tracks papers that apply **deep learning** to *de novo* peptide sequencing. It is **not** a historical survey of the field — pre-deep-learning approaches (PEAKS, Lutefisk, PepNovo, NovoHMM, …) are deliberately out of scope. Want a paper added? See [Contributing](#contributing).
:::

## The wave

The first deep-learning paper for *de novo* peptide sequencing appeared in **${first_year}**. Activity has accelerated sharply since — most published work is still preprints, reflecting how young this corner of the field is.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 50,
  height: 360,
  x: { label: "Year", tickFormat: "d", interval: 1 },
  y: { label: "Papers", grid: true },
  color: { legend: true, scheme: "blues", domain: ["preprint", "peer-reviewed", "ML conference", "thesis"] },
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(
      pubs_t.filter(p => Number.isFinite(p.year)),
      Plot.groupX(
        { y: "count" },
        { x: "year", fill: "type", tip: true }
      )
    ),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})
```

## The architectures

Deep-learning *de novo* sequencing has cycled through several architectural families. Toggle a family to focus on its lineage; hover a dot to read the model's signature contribution.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof family_filter = Inputs.checkbox(
  families,
  { label: "Families", value: families }
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
innovations_timeline = {
  // Notebook-style band order: oldest paradigm at the bottom, newest at the top.
  const band_order = ["CNN + RNN", "Transformer (AR)", "GNN", "CNN", "Transformer (NAR)", "Diffusion"]
  const band_color = {
    "CNN + RNN":         "#4C72B0",
    "Transformer (AR)":  "#DD8452",
    "GNN":               "#937860",
    "CNN":               "#8172B3",
    "Transformer (NAR)": "#55A868",
    "Diffusion":         "#C44E52"
  }
  // Tiers chosen to spread labels both above and below the band center.
  const Y_TIERS = [0.5, 0.8, 0.2, 0.9, 0.1, 0.65, 0.35, 0.95, 0.05, 0.72, 0.28, 0.85, 0.15, 0.58, 0.42]
  const MIN_GAP_DAYS = 180

  // Only include bands actually represented in the data AND surviving the family-filter checkboxes.
  const present = new Set(algorithms_t.filter(a => a.first_pub && a.family).map(a => a.family))
  const visible_bands = band_order.filter(f => present.has(f) && family_filter.includes(f))

  // Each band gets unit height, ordered bottom-to-top.
  const bands = visible_bands.map((family, i) => ({ family, y0: i, y1: i + 1, center: i + 0.5 }))
  const band_index = new Map(bands.map(b => [b.family, b]))

  // Assign each model a tier so labels at similar dates don't overlap.
  const rows = algorithms_t
    .filter(a => a.first_pub && band_index.has(a.family))
    .slice()
    .sort((a, b) => a.first_pub - b.first_pub)
  const last_date_at_tier = new Map()
  const items = []
  for (const row of rows) {
    let placed = false
    for (let ti = 0; ti < Y_TIERS.length; ti++) {
      const key = `${row.family}|${ti}`
      const last = last_date_at_tier.get(key)
      if (last === undefined || (row.first_pub - last) / 86400000 >= MIN_GAP_DAYS) {
        last_date_at_tier.set(key, row.first_pub)
        const band = band_index.get(row.family)
        items.push({ ...row, y: band.y0 + Y_TIERS[ti], y_frac: Y_TIERS[ti] })
        placed = true
        break
      }
    }
    if (!placed) {
      const band = band_index.get(row.family)
      items.push({ ...row, y: band.y0 + 0.5, y_frac: 0.5 })
    }
  }

  const all_dates = algorithms_t.filter(a => a.first_pub).map(a => a.first_pub)
  const x_min = d3.min(all_dates)
  const x_max = d3.max(all_dates)

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 1400,
    height: Math.max(420, bands.length * 110),
    marginLeft: 140,
    marginRight: 60,
    marginBottom: 40,
    x: { type: "time", label: "First publication →", domain: [d3.timeMonth.offset(x_min, -3), d3.timeMonth.offset(x_max, 3)], grid: true },
    y: { domain: [0, bands.length], axis: null },
    color: { domain: band_order, range: band_order.map(f => band_color[f]), legend: false },
    marks: [
      // Background band per family (tinted)
      Plot.rect(bands, {
        x1: () => x_min, x2: () => x_max,
        y1: "y0", y2: "y1",
        fill: "family",
        fillOpacity: 0.09
      }),
      // Thin separators between bands
      Plot.ruleY(bands.flatMap(b => [b.y0, b.y1]), { stroke: "#ddd", strokeWidth: 0.5 }),
      // Left-edge family label
      Plot.text(bands, {
        x: () => d3.timeMonth.offset(x_min, -3),
        y: "center",
        text: "family",
        textAnchor: "end",
        dx: -8,
        fontSize: 12,
        fontWeight: "bold",
        fill: "family"
      }),
      // Dots
      Plot.dot(items, {
        x: "first_pub",
        y: "y",
        fill: "family",
        r: 7,
        stroke: "white",
        strokeWidth: 1.5,
        tip: true,
        title: d => `${d.model}\n${d.family}\n${d.description ?? ""}\n${d.first_pub.toISOString().slice(0,10)}`
      }),
      // Labels offset above or below the dot depending on its band-fraction
      Plot.text(items, {
        x: "first_pub",
        y: "y",
        text: "model",
        dy: d => d.y_frac >= 0.5 ? -12 : 12,
        fontSize: 10,
        fontWeight: "bold",
        fill: "family"
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

## Where the work happens

Authors and the institutions behind them span **${n_countries}** countries.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof country_metric = Inputs.radio(
  ["authors", "affiliations"],
  { label: "Show", value: "authors" }
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 140,
  height: Math.max(280, geo_t.length * 22),
  x: { label: country_metric === "authors" ? "Distinct authors" : "Distinct affiliations", grid: true },
  y: { label: null },
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(geo_t, {
      x: country_metric,
      y: "country",
      fill: "steelblue",
      sort: { y: "x", reverse: true },
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

### Top institutions

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 280,
  height: Math.max(260, institutions_t.length * 26),
  x: { label: "Distinct authors", grid: true },
  y: { label: null },
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(institutions_t, {
      x: "authors",
      y: d => `${d.institution} — ${d.country}`,
      fill: "country",
      sort: { y: "x", reverse: true },
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

## Who's driving it

The chart shows the twenty most-published authors. The network below it shows how authors with **≥ 3 papers** are connected through co-authorship; drag a node to reshape the layout, or hover to highlight a neighborhood.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 180,
  height: Math.max(260, top_authors_t.length * 22),
  x: { label: "Papers", grid: true },
  y: { label: null },
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(top_authors_t, {
      x: "publications",
      y: "name",
      fill: "#1f6feb",
      sort: { y: "x", reverse: true },
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
network_chart = {
  const width = 1000
  const height = 600

  // Author → primary affiliation (most common one)
  const aff_by_author = d3.rollup(
    author_affs_t,
    v => v[0].affiliation,
    d => d.author
  )

  const nodes_by_name = new Map()
  for (const e of coauth_edges_t) {
    for (const n of [e.source, e.target]) {
      if (!nodes_by_name.has(n)) {
        nodes_by_name.set(n, { id: n, affiliation: aff_by_author.get(n) ?? "Unknown", degree: 0 })
      }
    }
    nodes_by_name.get(e.source).degree += e.weight
    nodes_by_name.get(e.target).degree += e.weight
  }
  const nodes = Array.from(nodes_by_name.values())
  const links = coauth_edges_t.map(e => ({ source: e.source, target: e.target, weight: e.weight }))

  const affs = Array.from(new Set(nodes.map(n => n.affiliation))).sort()
  const color = d3.scaleOrdinal(d3.schemeTableau10.concat(d3.schemeSet3)).domain(affs)

  const sim = d3.forceSimulation(nodes)
    .force("link", d3.forceLink(links).id(d => d.id).distance(60).strength(d => 0.1 + 0.05 * d.weight))
    .force("charge", d3.forceManyBody().strength(-180))
    .force("center", d3.forceCenter(width / 2, height / 2))
    .force("collide", d3.forceCollide().radius(d => 4 + Math.sqrt(d.degree)))

  const svg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", [0, 0, width, height])
    .attr("style", "max-width: 100%; height: auto; font: 10px sans-serif; cursor: grab;")

  const g = svg.append("g")

  svg.call(d3.zoom().on("zoom", ev => g.attr("transform", ev.transform)))

  const link = g.append("g")
    .attr("stroke-opacity", 0.4)
    .selectAll("line")
    .data(links)
    .join("line")
    .attr("stroke", "#999")
    .attr("stroke-width", d => Math.sqrt(d.weight))

  const node = g.append("g")
    .selectAll("circle")
    .data(nodes)
    .join("circle")
    .attr("r", d => 4 + Math.sqrt(d.degree))
    .attr("fill", d => color(d.affiliation))
    .attr("stroke", "#fff")
    .attr("stroke-width", 1.2)
    .call(drag(sim))

  node.append("title").text(d => `${d.id}\n${d.affiliation}\n${d.degree} co-author links`)

  const label = g.append("g")
    .attr("pointer-events", "none")
    .selectAll("text")
    .data(nodes)
    .join("text")
    .text(d => d.id)
    .attr("dx", 7)
    .attr("dy", 3)
    .attr("font-size", 9)
    .attr("fill", "#222")

  sim.on("tick", () => {
    link
      .attr("x1", d => d.source.x).attr("y1", d => d.source.y)
      .attr("x2", d => d.target.x).attr("y2", d => d.target.y)
    node.attr("cx", d => d.x).attr("cy", d => d.y)
    label.attr("x", d => d.x).attr("y", d => d.y)
  })

  invalidation.then(() => sim.stop())

  function drag(simulation) {
    return d3.drag()
      .on("start", (ev, d) => { if (!ev.active) simulation.alphaTarget(0.3).restart(); d.fx = d.x; d.fy = d.y })
      .on("drag", (ev, d) => { d.fx = ev.x; d.fy = ev.y })
      .on("end", (ev, d) => { if (!ev.active) simulation.alphaTarget(0); d.fx = null; d.fy = null })
  }

  return svg.node()
}
```

## Where it appears

Most papers in this space appear first on bioRxiv or arXiv. Toggle preprints vs. peer-reviewed to see how the venue distribution shifts.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof venue_type = Inputs.radio(
  ["all", "preprint", "peer-reviewed"],
  { label: "Publication type", value: "all" }
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const filtered = venue_type === "all" ? venues_t : venues_t.filter(v => v.type === venue_type)
  const grouped = Array.from(
    d3.rollup(filtered, vs => d3.sum(vs, v => v.papers), v => v.venue),
    ([venue, papers]) => ({ venue, papers })
  ).sort((a, b) => d3.descending(a.papers, b.papers)).slice(0, 15)

  return Plot.plot({
    marginLeft: 180,
    height: Math.max(260, grouped.length * 24),
    x: { label: "Papers", grid: true },
    y: { label: null },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(grouped, {
        x: "papers",
        y: "venue",
        fill: "#6f42c1",
        sort: { y: "x", reverse: true },
        tip: true
      }),
      Plot.ruleX([0])
    ]
  })
}
```

## Browse all papers

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof search = Inputs.search(pubs_t, { placeholder: `Search ${n_papers} papers…` })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(search, {
  columns: ["year", "models", "title", "authors", "journal", "type", "doi"],
  header: { year: "Year", models: "Model(s)", title: "Title", authors: "Authors", journal: "Venue", type: "Type", doi: "DOI" },
  format: {
    title: (t, i) => {
      const row = search[i]
      const url = row?.url || (row?.doi ? `https://doi.org/${row.doi}` : null)
      return url ? htl.html`<a href="${url}" target="_blank" rel="noopener">${t}</a>` : t
    },
    authors: a => a && a.length > 80 ? a.slice(0, 80) + "…" : a,
    doi: d => d ? htl.html`<a href="https://doi.org/${d}" target="_blank" rel="noopener">${d}</a>` : ""
  },
  sort: "year",
  reverse: true,
  rows: 25,
  width: { year: 60, type: 100, models: 130 }
})
```

## Contributing {#contributing}

This site is generated from a small SQLite database (`denovo.db`), seeded from `related_literature.csv` via `populate_remaining.py`. To propose a new paper:

1. Add a row to `related_literature.csv` with model name, title, authors, date, publisher, and (if available) repository / model weights / PDF URL.
2. Run `uv run python populate_remaining.py` to ingest, then `sqlite3 denovo.db .dump > denovo.sql` to regenerate the human-readable dump.
3. Open a PR. The GitHub Action will rebuild the site and publish to `gh-pages` on merge.

---

::: {.text-muted}
This page tracks **deep-learning** approaches to *de novo* peptide sequencing only. Pre-deep-learning methods are out of scope. Source data and code: [GitHub](https://github.com/BioGeek/awesome_de_novo_peptide_sequencing) — rebuilt automatically on every push to `main`.
:::
